name: Climate Bonds Standard
description: The Climate Bonds Standard and Certification Scheme is an investor-focused labelling scheme for bonds and loans. It allows investors and intermediaries to easily assess whether bonds and loans are aligned with the goals of the Paris Agreement. Version 4.0 introduces enhanced requirements for transition finance and entity-level credibility.

certificationProcess.overview: The Climate Bonds Certification is a two-step process: pre-issuance certification (confirming intended use of proceeds and framework alignment) and post-issuance certification (confirming actual allocation of proceeds).

steps.name: Pre-Issuance Certification
steps.description: Before or at the time of issuance. Issuer appoints an Approved Verifier who assesses conformance with the Climate Bonds Standard, including proposed use of proceeds and alignment with sector-specific criteria.
requirements: Nominated projects and assets meet applicable Sector Criteria
Internal processes and controls for tracking, managing proceeds, and reporting are adequate
Issuer's climate strategy and transition plan assessed (v4.0 requirement)

steps.name: Post-Issuance Certification
steps.description: Within 24 months of issuance. Issuer confirms that proceeds have been allocated as described in the pre-issuance application.
requirements: Proceeds allocated to eligible green projects/assets
Allocation reporting completed
Ongoing compliance with Sector Criteria confirmed
Post-issuance verification report from Approved Verifier

certificationProcess.approvedVerifiers: CBI maintains a list of Approved Verifiers who are authorised to conduct Climate Bonds verification. These are typically major audit/assurance firms and specialist ESG verifiers.
certificationProcess.certificationMark: Certified Climate Bond — use of the certification mark is conditional on maintaining post-issuance compliance.
certificationProcess.fees: Certification fees are based on the size of the issuance and range from $1,000 to $10,000+ depending on deal size.

sectorCriteria.description: The Climate Bonds Taxonomy provides sector-specific eligibility criteria. Each sector has detailed technical criteria that projects/assets must meet to qualify for certification.

sectors.name: Solar Energy
eligibleActivities: Dedicated solar energy infrastructure (grid connection, transmission)

criteria.automaticEligibility: All solar PV and CSP generation facilities are automatically eligible. No technology-specific thresholds apply.
exclusions: Facilities co-located with fossil fuel generation (unless clearly separated)
Solar facilities on land cleared of high carbon stock/high conservation value areas after 2020

sectors.name: Wind Energy
eligibleActivities: Dedicated wind energy infrastructure (grid connection, substations)

criteria.automaticEligibility: All onshore and offshore wind generation facilities are automatically eligible.
exclusions: Facilities that have not completed Environmental Impact Assessment where required

sectors.name: Buildings

newBuildings.requirement: Must be within the top 15% of building stock by carbon emissions in the local jurisdiction, OR meet a recognised green building certification (minimum level specified per certification system).

existingBuildings.requirement: Must be within the top 15% of building stock, OR achieve at least a 30% improvement in energy intensity through retrofit.

upgrades.requirement: Must achieve a minimum 30% improvement in energy performance or carbon intensity.

reporting: Energy Performance Certificate (EPC) rating or equivalent

sectors.name: Low Carbon Transport
eligibleActivities: Electric and hydrogen vehicles and charging infrastructure
Public passenger transport (bus, rail, metro, tram)

privateVehicles.requirement: Zero direct (tailpipe) emissions only (BEV, FCEV). ICE and conventional hybrids are excluded.

publicTransport.buses: Zero direct emissions, OR meeting specified gCO2/pkm threshold declining over time
publicTransport.rail: Electrified rail is automatically eligible. Diesel rail excluded unless part of an electrification programme.

shipping.requirement: Must demonstrate emissions intensity at least 40% below the IMO 2008 baseline, declining to zero by 2050.

waterTreatment.requirement: Facility must meet applicable environmental discharge standards. Energy efficiency measures should be in place or planned.
waterTreatment.exclusion: Desalination plants powered predominantly by fossil fuels are excluded unless they meet specific energy efficiency criteria.

floodDefence.requirement: Projects must be informed by climate risk assessments incorporating future climate projections.

v4Enhancements.description: Version 4.0 of the Climate Bonds Standard introduced significant enhancements focused on entity-level credibility and transition planning.

transitionPlan.description: Issuers must have a credible entity-level transition plan aligned with 1.5°C pathways.
requirements: Paris-aligned GHG emission reduction targets covering Scope 1, 2 and material Scope 3
Short-term (5 years), medium-term (2030) and long-term (2050) targets
Decarbonisation strategy and actions to achieve targets
Governance arrangements for transition plan oversight
Disclosure of transition plan in accordance with recognised frameworks (e.g., TCFD, ISSB)

noNewFossilFuels.description: Issuers cannot be expanding fossil fuel production or reserves.
requirements: No new fossil fuel exploration or production projects
No expansion of existing fossil fuel extraction capacity
Phase-out plan for existing fossil fuel activities (if applicable)

lobbyingAlignment.description: Issuer's lobbying activities must be aligned with Paris Agreement goals.
requirements: No lobbying against climate policy
Disclosure of trade association memberships and alignment assessment

transitionLabels.description: v4.0 introduces specific provisions for transition finance — recognising that hard-to-abate sectors need pathways to decarbonise.
requirements: Entity-level transition plan meeting CBI requirements
Sector-specific pathway alignment
Measurable interim targets
Independent verification of transition plan credibility

postIssuanceReporting.frequency: Annual until proceeds are fully allocated, then at least annually for the life of the bond

details: Total proceeds allocated per eligible category
Confirmation that nominated projects/assets continue to meet Sector Criteria
Any changes to the project/asset portfolio
Sector-specific impact metrics as defined in Sector Criteria
Methodology and assumptions used for impact calculations
Key actions taken in the reporting period
